reviews.review_msg: Genuinely speaking, Bingham is a one of a kind academic inspiration unlike any other that I have ever witnessed in undergrad. Lecture concepts share a common philosophical thread that all tie into the course themes in which he explains and reiterates thoroughly throughout the semester. Workload was quite easy and the marking was reasonable.

Lectures are really engaging and the course concepts are really interesting. Weekly readings and logs and a project for grading, but very do-able and you have a lot of freedom to write about what you want as long as you refer to readings and lecture. Funny guy but rambles a lot. Lecture is mandatory as you have to input attendance to get a grade.

Charles is the most wholesome professor. He is so kind hearted and truly cares about his students. He is the best professor I have had at SFU and he makes me feel like this is how school and teachers should all be like. Caring of your mental health, understanding, and patient. He understands that we are just human and we make mistakes.
Absolutely worst course I have ever taken anywhere and I have had some doozies! Her fascist take on women's history made me embarassed to be both a feminist and a lesbian. Avoid at all costs.
Very Biased and unhelpful, if you like history DO NOT take a class with her!

This instructor is the reason I have dropped my history major. Absolutely worst instructor in my three years at SFU. Biased and obnoxious. Students who disputed her peurile feminist version of history
 were literally shouted into silence.

Julia is a very good instructor with patience. She welcome various of different opinions. It is very pleasant to attend her lecture. The advice she gives you for your paper is really helpful. She is awesome!

Amyn himself is great. Approachable, easy-going, and fair. This course...well, SFU should reconsider it as a mandatory one. LOTS of assignments and heavy on the attendance component. Group projects also make up a good chunk of your grade so pray you get a group who cares.

I had a really fun time learning in Bus 240 thanks to Mr. Somani. His lectures were always engaging and he was always helpful when answering questions or concerns. Overall the course was good and it was a pleasure to learn from him.

Whole class is negotiating with TAs to get a better grade. Because your grade can always increase a few percent by bargaining with the TAs. Why not just give me those percentages the first time marked? Idiocracy at its finest.

Amyn himself is a good guy, good prof. 240 is just a bad course, but it's new, so it may be improved over time. The lectures were hit & miss, but could be fun sometimes. Pray you get a good group (40% of grade). There were also big differences in how both TA's marked assignments, which caused drama. Most assignments were also pretty confusing.

Amyn was a decent prof however it was hard to stay focused in class at times. There are no exams just two individual and two team assignments but they were a bit time-consuming. My only concern was the curve is really high which can make it hard to do well and you would often need to go to the Ta for a regrade.

He loves to talk about how great he is for most of the class. Group projects make up most of the grade so pray for a good group. If you dont come to class he will deduct marks for the next assignment. Class can be fun at times, but is pretty useless.
